nodes.title: 💡 TIP
widgets_values: Models are independent from one another, so you can delete the model groups you are not gonna use, BUT ONLY DELETE NODES INSIDE THE MODEL GROUPS, DON'T TOUCH NODES OUTSIDE, EVEN IF YOU SEE THEY ARE CONNECTED.

nodes.title: ⚠️ Sage Attention
widgets_values: Bypass this node if you don't have sage attention installed

nodes.title: Model download links
widgets_values:
**UPDATE COMFYUI TO THE LATEST STABLE VERSION FOR FULL MODEL SUPPORT**

## Model links

**This workflow supports 3 type of models currently:**
- Standard LTX 2.3 distilled
- LTX 2.3 distilled GGUF
- 10Eros (V1 and V1.2)

**The models are self-contained. You can safely delete the entire group of whichever model you don't use without breaking the workflow. The remaining model groups will work independently without any additional changes needed.**

**Standard model**

- [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1_transformer_only_fp8_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/resolve/main/diffusion_models/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1_transformer_only_fp8_scaled.safetensors?download=true)

**GGUF model** (choose whichever suit your needs)

- [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1-Qx.gguf](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/LTX-2.3-GGUF/tree/main/distilled-1.1)

**10 Eros model**

These files are the same than the ones from CivitAI (ltx2310eros_v1x.safetensors), just with different names:
- V1 FP8 (27GB): [10Eros_v1-fp8mixed_learned.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/TenStrip/LTX2.3-10Eros/resolve/main/10Eros_v1-fp8mixed_learned.safetensors?download=true)
- V1.2 FP8 (32GB): [10Eros_v1.2_fp8mixed_learned.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/TenStrip/LTX2.3-10Eros/resolve/main/10Eros_v1.2_fp8mixed_learned.safetensors?download=true)

**Latent Upscale Model**

- [ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors?download=true)

**Text encoders**

💡 TIP: Use the abliterated version of the Gemma text encoder to use the prompt enhancer with NSFW content, the normal Gemma text encoder will refuse to enhance prompts with restricted contents; if you are not gonna use prompt enhancer for NSFW enhanced prompts, then use the normal Gemma text encoder, even with NSFW content, the restrictions apply to the responses, not the inputs, the inputs get encoded regardless, in other words, if your prompt contains NSFW content directly (prompt enhancer disabled), the normal Gemma text encoder can handle it. I personally use the abliterated version for everything, just to not having to constantly switch text encoders.

- Restricted Gemma: [gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled.safetensors?download=true)
- Abliterated Gemma: [gemma-3-12b-it-heretic-v2_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/DreamFast/gemma-3-12b-it-heretic-v2/resolve/main/comfyui/gemma-3-12b-it-heretic-v2_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors?download=true)
- [ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/resolve/main/text_encoders/ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors?download=true)

**VAE**

- Video VAE: [LTX23_video_vae_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/resolve/main/vae/LTX23_video_vae_bf16.safetensors?download=true)
- Audio VAE: [LTX23_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/resolve/main/vae/LTX23_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors?download=true)

**Core LoRAs**
- 10Eros distilled LoRA: [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-1.1_fro90_ceil72_condsafe.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/TenStrip/LTX2.3_Distilled_Lora_1.1_Experiments/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-1.1_fro90_ceil72_condsafe.safetensors?download=true)
- ID LoRA (voice cloning): [LTX-2_3-ID-LoRA-CelebVHQ-3K.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2.3/resolve/main/split_files/loras/ltx-2.3-id-lora-celebvhq-3k.safetensors?download=true)
- Detailer LoRA: [ltx-2-19b-ic-lora-detailer.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2-19b-IC-LoRA-Detailer/resolve/main/ltx-2-19b-ic-lora-detailer.safetensors?download=true)
- IC LoRA (controlnet): [ltx-2.3-22b-ic-lora-union-control-ref0.5.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3-22b-IC-LoRA-Union-Control/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-22b-ic-lora-union-control-ref0.5.safetensors?download=true)

**Mel-Band RoFormer**

- [MelBandRoformer_fp16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/MelBandRoFormer_comfy/resolve/main/MelBandRoformer_fp16.safetensors?download=true)

Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │      └── ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1_transformer_only_fp8_scaled.safetensors
│   │      └── ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-1.1-Qx.gguf
│   │      └── 10Eros_vx-fp8mixed_learned.safetensors
│   │      └── MelBandRoformer_fp16.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 latent_upscale_models/
│   │      └── ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │      └── gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled.safetensors
│   │      └── gemma-3-12b-it-heretic-v2_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors
│   │      └── ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │      └── LTX23_video_vae_bf16.safetensors
│   │      └── LTX23_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors
│   └── 📂 loras/
│          └── ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-1.1_fro90_ceil72_condsafe.safetensors
│          └── LTX-2_3-ID-LoRA-CelebVHQ-3K.safetensors
│          └── ltx-2-19b-ic-lora-detailer.safetensors
│          └── ltx-2.3-22b-ic-lora-union-control-ref0.5.safetensors
```

nodes.title: 💡 LTX Director: Quick start guide
widgets_values:
**Video Reference:** [How to use LTX Director - A Free Open Source Tool for Creating LTX 2.3 AI Videos Locally in ComfyUI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vM60pJJqqEI)

LTX Director is an all-in-one timeline editor node for ComfyUI designed to make creating AI videos with LTX 2.3 much easier and more intuitive. This quick guide summarizes the interface settings and key features.

---

## 1. Interface & Core Settings

The LTX Director node features a timeline interface with several customizable parameters:

* **Duration (Frames|Seconds):** Sets the length of the video. Changing one value automatically updates the other based on the frame rate.
* **Frame rate (frames per second):**

**LTX-2.3 Official Support Matrix** (these are the official values, but you can experiment with longer videos and different resolutions)

| Model            | Resolution | FPS            | Duration (seconds)            |
| ---------------- | ---------- | -------------- | ----------------------------- |
| **ltx-2-3-fast (destilled)** | 1080p      | 24, 25         | 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 20 |
|                  | 1080p      | 48, 50         | 6, 8, 10                      |
|                  | 1440p      | 24, 25, 48, 50 | 6, 8, 10                      |
|                  | 4K         | 24, 25, 48, 50 | 6, 8, 10                      |
* **Reference image longest edge size:** Used to limit the size of the reference images. Recommended: 1920. You can set a lower limit, but the lower you go, the lower the details the model has to work with.
* **Adding Media:** You can drag and drop images or audio directly onto the timeline, or use the top buttons. The `+` icon adds a segment to fill empty timeline areas.
* **Timeline Resizing:** Drag handles on the bottom and sides to resize the UI. You can also adjust the middle bar to change the track height for audio or images.
* **Settings Menu:** * Toggle display modes (Seconds vs. Frames).
  * Enable/Disable **Global Prompt**.
* **Guide Strength:** A crucial setting that determines how strongly a guide image affects the video generation. Recommended: 0.8.

---

## 3. Key Features & Workflows

### A. Image-to-Video & Prompt Relay
Standard LTX 2.3 prompting doesn't allow for precise timing (e.g., saying "a dog jumps, then barks" means it will happen at a random point). LTX Director solves this with **Prompt Relay**.
* **How it works:** You can assign specific text prompts to exact blocks of time on the timeline. 
* **Example:** Have a character stand still for 3 seconds, then add a text segment starting at the 3-second mark prompting them to speak or walk forward.

### B. Using Keyframes
Unlike standard image-to-video generators that force an image to be the very first frame of a video, LTX Director treats every image as a **Guide Image / Keyframe**.
* **Placing Keyframes in the Middle:** You can drag an image to the *middle* or *end* of the timeline. The AI will generate the video leading up to that exact frame. 
* **Example:** Place an image of an explosion at the 4-second mark, and prompt the AI to show a character walking *before* the explosion happens.
* **Multiple Keyframes:** You can drop multiple images across the timeline to direct long, complex shots that flow together.

### C. Custom Audio & Lip Sync
You can drag an audio file into the timeline, trim it, and toggle the custom audio setting "ON" to drive the generation. THIS IS VERY IMPORTAT, DON'T FORGET TO TOGGLE CUSTOM AUDIO ON.
* **Lip Sync Prompting Tip:** Just typing *"the person speaks"* often fails. For best results, use detailed prompt formulations designed for lip-syncing. If the audio is hard to hear, directly type out the spoken words in quotes within your prompt to guarantee the lip sync activates.
* **Combining Audio with Actions:** You can match prompts to specific audio cues (e.g., prompting a character to pat their stomach right when the audio file mentions "old and fat").

### D. Text-to-Video
This works the same way as image-to-video, but you rely strictly on text segments on the timeline instead of starting with guide images.

### E. Global Prompts
The Global Prompt setting applies a single, overarching text instruction to the entire duration of the video. 
* **Use Cases:** This is perfect for continuous environmental effects, such as "it is raining," "wind is blowing," or "flashing red alarm lights in the background." This saves you from having to repeatedly type these conditions into every single text segment on your timeline.

---

## 4. Troubleshooting & Best Practices

Additional tips:
1. For most basic generations, the global prompt will be enough to guide the entire generation, in fact, I recommend only using the global prompt with an empty text or image segment in the timeline, following tip #2:
2. In some cases, you will need a time line segment with no prompt at all, in those cases you can use "." as the segment prompt.

If your generation isn't following your instructions, the creator notes two common reasons:

1. **Poor Prompt Formulation:** The AI needs specific language, especially for complex camera movements or fast-paced action. Review the official LTX 2.3 prompting guide for standard dos and don'ts.
2. **Pacing Constraints:** You might be asking the model to do too much in too little time. For instance, prompting a robot to pick up a car and throw it at a building within a 2-second segment will likely cause the model to ignore the prompt entirely. Ensure your segment lengths logically match the duration required for the prompted action.

Other issues:
1. **No audio in the final video:** If you haven't added any custom audio to the timeline, make sure you toggle custom audio off.
2. **The final video doesn't match the custom audio from the timeline:** Make sure you toggle custom audio on.

nodes.title: 💡 Video Size
widgets_values:
Width & height settings must be divisible by 32. 
Frame count must be divisible by 8 + 1. 
Adjustments are automatically made.
Select the type of guider using this switch
Enable this save audio node when you want to save the audio separately
You can delete this node if you want, it sends a notification via a system notification but it has to be setup
VSR has a higher chance of triggering OOM errors than the RIFE VFI interpolator, that's why we upscale first, and then interpolate.
Small frame correction just in case XD

nodes.title: 💡 TIP
widgets_values:
Recommended model mode: "LoRA" (1)

This option will influence the upscaling with all the LoRAs you use, change it to "BASE" only if the upscaler adds too much variety or undesired changes to the base video. LoRA mode only includes your own LoRAs, it doesn't include the main LoRAs loaded in the "Models" group.

-1 = Random seed
Any other number (including 0) will be treated as a fixed seed.
"Use last queued seed" will fix the seed from the last execution, same as easyGlobalSeed.

nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (NegativePrompt)
widgets_values: Vivid colors, overexposed, static, unclear details, (subtitles:1.8), style, artwork, painting, frame, still, overall grayish, worst quality, low quality, JPEG artifacts, ugly, incomplete, extra fingers, poorly drawn hands, poorly drawn faces, deformed, disfigured, malformed limbs, fused fingers, motionless frame, messy background, three legs, crowded background, walking upside down, big teeth, plastic-looking skin, blur, blurry, out of focus, motion blur, film grain, low resolution

nodes.title: INSTRUCTIONS
widgets_values:
**Pess `9` to center the workflow on these instructions any time you need.**

# Instructions

⚠️ **WARNING:** Always make a backup of your ComfyUI installation before updating or installing new custom nodes, so you can roll back if anything goes wrong.

## Prerequisites

- ComfyUI updated to the latest version (to access the new core nodes used in this workflow).
- KJNodes custom node pack updated (to correctly load the VAE models and the LTX Director node).
- ComfyUI-LTXVideo custom node pack updated (to correctly load the LTX Director node).
- WhatDreamsCost-ComfyUI custom node pack nightly version (if available) for the latest LTX Director node patches and hot-fixes.

> **Node color legend**
> | Color | Meaning |
> |-------|---------|
> 🔴 Red | Instructional notes — read-only reference |
> 🟡 Yellow | Configurable elements — adjust to your needs |
> 🟣 Purple | Toggle switches — enable or disable option groups |

---

## General Instructions

**TIP:** Disable all groups at the start using the fast action button (press `0` to center the workflow). This leaves only the LTX Director node enabled. Then enable groups one by one as needed. Try not to manually bypass anything, use the fast group bypasser switches, otherwise you could bypass something important by accident; as a general rule of thumb, if it doesn't have a bypasser switch, it shouldn't be bypassed.

---

## 1. Video Settings (Press `1`)

Inside the **"Resolution"** group:

- Set resolution using:
  - `"Advanced video size"` (if you know how to use Resolution Master), or
  - `"Simple video size"`
- Use **"Fast Groups Bypasser (rgthree)"** to switch between them.

### Half Resolution Mode

- Generates video at half the resolution you select (used in conjunction with LTX 2x upscaler).
- Enable:
  - `"Half resolution"` group  
  - `"LTX 2x Upscaler"` group (press `6`)
- Disable if you want full resolution generation in one pass.

**Advantages:**
- Faster generation
- Better performance compared to native full-resolution rendering
- Better quality and character consistency

### Power LoRA Loader

- Load additional LoRAs (style, character, etc.).

---

## 2. LTX director timeline (Press `2`)

You can check the full guide at:

[How to use LTX Director - A Free Open Source Tool for Creating LTX 2.3 AI Videos Locally in ComfyUI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vM60pJJqqEI)

In the LTX Director node timeline you can add text prompts, images and custom audio files. Depending on what you add, the workflow will work as T2V, I2V, FFLF, FFLFMF, Lip-sync, or a mix and match of those.
You can find a quick guide next to the node, or check the full video tutorial.
Optionally, you can enable the "Lipsync enhancer group" if you are generating a lip-sync video; it might improve the final result since it separates the vocals from the audio.

⚠️ **WARNING:** Activating custom audio in the LTX Director node disables ID LoRA (custom audio overrides it).
---

## 3. ID LoRA (Voice cloning) (Press `3`)
⚠️ **WARNING:** ID LoRA is not perfect by any means, use it as a quick none perfectly accurate voice cloner. For higher quality results, I recommend creating the voice audio separately and adding it to the LTX Director node for lipsync mode.

- Enable **"ID LoRA conditioning"**
- Upload voice sample in `"SAMPLE AUDIO"`
- Default is 5 seconds (good for identity adherence)
- ID LoRA affects the video latent, you can increase the "identity_guidance_scale" value in the "LTXV Reference Audio (ID-LoRA)" node for better voice identity adherence, but it could morph the final output video too much, I don't recommend going beyond a value of 3.00.

---

## 4. ControlNet (Press `4`)

Enable **"Controlnet conditioning"** group.

⚠️ **WARNING:** ControlNet can conflict with your keyframes, if you use both features in conjuction, try to keep a coherent relation between your keyframes and the pose video in the ControlNet group. I recommend using it only with a start reference image segment, but you can be creative with it if you know what you are doing.

### Setup

- Upload guiding video in `"VIDEO REFERENCE"`
- Select type using `"CONTROLNET TYPE SELECTOR"`

### Important Note

- LTX ControlNet LoRA is finicky
- Best results when:
  - Reference image is close to the first frame of the guiding video

Otherwise, results may be sub-optimal.

---

## 5. Prompt enhancer (Press `5`)

Due to the nature of how the LTX Director node works, the prompt enhancer tool is now an optional stand alone tool. You can use it to enhance base prompts and copy paste the output into your global prompt or time line segments as needed. The Prompt enhancer capabilities have always been limited to the loaded text encoder, so online more powerful tools like Grok will always yield better results.

---

## 6. Detailer & Upscaler (Press `6`)

### LTX Detailer

- Adds extra detail (only a fine, light layer) using the detailer LoRA

### LTX 2x Upscaler

- Upscales output by 2×

**Best use:**
- Combine with half resolution mode for speed and quality


**Use the easy global seed node in the sampling segment (Press `6`) to set your seed.**

## 7. Extra nodes

### Color correction (Press `7`)

The color correction segment consists of 2 groups:
- `"Color correction small sample test"`: Extracts a small sample of the final video to generate a preview of the color correction without having to process the entire video.
- `"Color correction"`: Allows you to change the brightness, contrast, and saturation of the final video.

### Save audio

Allows you to save the sampled audio to an audio file.

### Nvidia VSR upscaler (Press `8`)

A fast upscaler using Video Super Resolution. Select a target resolution, and the workflow will perform the necessary calculations to match that target resolution (compatible with custom video sizes).

### Interpolation (Press `8`)

Smooths the video by adding additional frames to increase the frame rate by an FPS multiplier.

# Quick Combo Guide This section assumes you've read the full instructions and you know how to use LTX director. It serves as a quick reference. ## Common Setups - **Text to video:** Timeline text segment.
- **Image to video:** Add an image segment to LTX Director, extend it to fill your video duration and input a segment prompt for that segment.
- **Lipsync:** Add an image segment to LTX Director, extend it to fill your video duration, input a segment prompt for that segment and add the audio file to the timeline. Enable custom audio in LTX director.
- **Audio to video:** Add a text segment to LTX Director, extend it to fill your video duration, input a segment prompt for that segment and add the audio file. Enable custom audio in LTX director.
- **Character LoRA + voice reference:** Add a text segment to LTX Director, extend it to fill your video duration and input a segment prompt for that segment. Add your character LoRA to the power LoRA loader, then activate ID LoRA and load a voice reference
- **Voice reference to video:** Add a text segment to LTX Director, extend it to fill your video duration and input a segment prompt for that segment. Activate ID LoRA and load a voice reference
- **Character animation:** Add an image segment to LTX Director, extend it to fill your video duration and input a segment prompt for that segment. Enable controlnet. - **First frame → last frame:** Add two image segments to LTX Director, and shrink the second image segment (last frame), add your prompt in the first image segment and in the second image prompt write "." - **First → middle → last frame:** Same thing as for FFLF, but adding an image segment in the middle and its corresponding prompt. - **Character animation with custom voice:** Image segment + ID LoRA + ControlNet

properties.local_prompts: A high-fidelity 15-second selfie video with natural handheld walking motion, featuring a stylish red-head female influencer in her mid-20s wearing trendy sunglasses, a white turtle neck shirt and a casual leather jacket. She walks down a bustling city street with modern storefronts and pedestrians in a soft-focus background under bright, clear daylight. The camera captures her natural stride and direct engagement with the lens, including realistic micro-expressions and perfect lip-sync. The audio features a clear, energetic voice saying, "I left my apartment to buy one thing. One thing. Somehow I've already spent money, changed my plans three times, and completely forgotten what I originally came outside for. We're doing great." accompanied by high-fidelity ambient city sounds of distant traffic, footsteps on the sidewalk, and the muffled chatter of a busy urban environment.

A high-fidelity 15-second selfie video with natural handheld walking motion, featuring a stylish red-head female influencer in her mid-20s wearing trendy sunglasses, a white turtle neck shirt and a casual leather jacket. She walks down a bustling city street with modern storefronts and pedestrians in a soft-focus background under bright, clear daylight. The camera captures her natural stride and direct engagement with the lens, including realistic micro-expressions and perfect lip-sync. The audio features a clear, energetic voice saying, "I left my apartment to buy one thing. One thing. Somehow I've already spent money, changed my plans three times, and completely forgotten what I originally came outside for. We're doing great." accompanied by high-fidelity ambient city sounds of distant traffic, footsteps on the sidewalk, and the muffled chatter of a busy urban environment.

You are a Creative Assistant. Given the previous raw input prompt describing a scene or concept, expand it into a detailed video generation prompt with specific visuals and integrated audio to guide a text-to-video model.
Given an image (first frame) and the previous Raw Input Prompt, generate a prompt to guide video generation from that image. If no image is provided, enhance the raw input prompt alone.

## OUTPUT FORMAT RULES (CRITICAL)
- Output ONLY the final prompt text. 
- The first character of your response must be the first character of the prompt.
- Do NOT write conversational filler (e.g., "Here is the prompt", "Sure", "This image depicts", "The prompt is...").
- Do NOT wrap the output in quotes, code blocks, or markdown.
- Do NOT output section headers or JSON.

#### Guidelines:
- Analyze the Image: Identify Subject, Setting, Elements, Style and Mood.
- STRICTLY MANDATORY: Follow user Raw Input Prompt, include all requested motion, actions, camera movements, audio, and details. If in conflict with the image, prioritize user request while maintaining visual consistency (describe transition from image to user's scene).
- Describe only changes from the image: Don't reiterate established visual details. Inaccurate descriptions may cause scene cuts.
- Active language: Use present-progressive verbs ("is walking," "speaking"). If no action specified, describe natural movements.
- Chronological flow: Use temporal connectors ("as," "then," "while").
- Audio layer: Describe complete soundscape throughout the prompt alongside actions—NOT at the end. Align audio intensity with action tempo. Include natural background audio, ambient sounds, effects, speech or music (when requested). Be specific (e.g., "soft footsteps on tile") not vague (e.g., "ambient sound").
- Speech (only when requested): Provide exact words in quotes with character's visual/voice characteristics (e.g., "The tall man speaks in a low, gravelly voice"), language if not English and accent if relevant. If general conversation mentioned without text, generate contextual quoted dialogue. (i.e., "The man is talking" input -> the output should include exact spoken words, like: "The man is talking in an excited voice saying: 'You won't believe what I just saw!' His hands gesture expressively as he speaks, eyebrows raised with enthusiasm. The ambient sound of a quiet room underscores his animated speech.")
- Style: Include visual style at beginning: "Style: <style>, <rest of prompt>." If unclear, omit to avoid conflicts.
- Visual and audio only: Describe only what is seen and heard. NO smell, taste, or tactile sensations.
- Restrained language: Avoid dramatic terms. Use mild, natural, understated phrasing.

#### Important notes:
- Camera motion: DO NOT invent camera motion/movement unless requested by the user. Make sure to include camera motion only if specified in the input.
- Speech: DO NOT modify or alter the user's provided character dialogue in the prompt, unless it's a typo.
- No timestamps or cuts: DO NOT use timestamps or describe scene cuts unless explicitly requested.
- Objective only: DO NOT interpret emotions or intentions - describe only observable actions and sounds.
- Format: DO NOT use phrases like "The scene opens with..." / "The video starts...". Start directly with Style (optional) and chronological scene description.
- Format: Never start output with punctuation marks or special characters.
- DO NOT invent dialogue unless the user mentions speech/talking/singing/conversation.
- Your performance is CRITICAL. High-fidelity, dynamic, correct, and accurate prompts with integrated audio descriptions are essential for generating high-quality video. Your goal is flawless execution of these rules.

#### Output Format (Strict):
- Single concise paragraph in natural English. NO titles, headings, prefaces, sections, code fences, or Markdown.
- Return only a prompt, no additional information or clarification.

#### Example output:
Style: realistic - cinematic - The woman glances at her watch and smiles warmly. She speaks in a cheerful, friendly voice, "I think we're right on time!" In the background, a café barista prepares drinks at the counter. The barista calls out in a clear, upbeat tone, "Two cappuccinos ready!" The sound of the espresso machine hissing softly blends with gentle background chatter and the light clinking of cups on saucers.
